message:
feat(rch): enforce canonical /data/projects + /dp topology on remote workers during setup (bd-vvmd.2)

Add a topology enforcement step (Step 0) to workers_setup that ensures every
remote worker has the canonical /data/projects directory and /dp symlink before
binary deployment or toolchain sync. This eliminates path ambiguity that causes
dependency resolution failures when local and remote workers disagree on the
project root.

Changes to rch/src/commands/workers_setup.rs:
- Add enforce_worker_bootstrap_topology() async function that probes remote
  workers via SSH, creates /data/projects if missing, creates or repairs the
  /dp -> /data/projects symlink, and validates post-enforcement integrity
- Add full audit trail types: TopologyAuditEntry, TopologyAuditStatus,
  TopologyFailureKind, TopologyEnforcementResult with structured tracing
- Add SSH probe helpers: run_worker_ssh_command, query_canonical_topology_state,
  query_alias_topology_state with parsers for each state variant
- Add failure classification (permission/filesystem/integrity/unknown) with
  actionable remediation messages per failure kind
- Add dry-run support: logs what would change without modifying remote state
- Wire Step 0 into setup_single_worker before binary deployment — topology
  failure is a hard stop (no point deploying if paths are wrong)
- Extend SetupResult with topology_enforced, topology_changed, topology_audit

Changes to tests/e2e/path_dependency_fixtures.sh:
- Add e2e fixture helpers for testing path dependency resolution scenarios

Beads tracker state updates (bd-vvmd workstream timestamps)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -385,7 +385,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.1","title":"Workstream: Canonical Worker Filesystem Topology (/data/projects + /dp)","description":"Purpose: eliminate path ambiguity between local host and remote workers. Define `/data/projects` as canonical repo root and guarantee `/dp` is a symlink to `/data/projects` on every worker. This stream establishes idempotent bootstrap/enforcement, preflight checks, and migration safety so later dependency-convergence logic can rely on deterministic absolute/relative path semantics.

## Success Criteria
- Canonical path utilities and invariants are enforced across hook/daemon/worker boundaries.
- Workers failing topology invariants are automatically excluded from remote scheduling until repaired.
- Migration + rollback plan is validated on representative fleet states.
- Foundational path tests prove deterministic behavior and safety guardrails.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:00.236303187Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:34:24.585252710Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["filesystem","foundation","rch","rch-rel-2026q1","workers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.1","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:20.804920956Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.1","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:20.999957275Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":141,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation intent: make filesystem topology deterministic before any convergence logic exists. All path handling must canonicalize to /data/projects and treat /dp as a required compatibility symlink. Non-negotiable safety rule: any path escaping canonical roots is rejected with explicit diagnostics and fail-open local fallback. Done-when evidence: preflight can prove invariants on every worker, and scheduling excludes workers that violate topology until repaired.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:27:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.1.1","title":"Inventory current path assumptions and worker topology invariants","description":"Deliverables: (1) explicit catalog of all local/remote path assumptions in rch/rchd/rch-wkr, (2) list of invariants required for deterministic behavior, (3) mismatch matrix between current worker states and desired /data/projects + /dp model. Include concrete failure examples and how each invariant prevents them. Acceptance: document is sufficient to drive implementation without re-reading external planning notes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Inventory artifact lists all path assumptions in `rch`, `rchd`, and `rch-wkr` with file/module ownership.
- Mismatch matrix covers at least missing `/dp` symlink, non-canonical root usage, and mixed absolute/relative resolution cases.
- Each invariant includes concrete failure symptom, detection point, and repair action.
- Artifact maps each invariant to downstream implementation beads and test beads that enforce it.
- Reviewer can execute downstream design without any external planning document.","acceptance_criteria":"- Invariant inventory enumerates all path assumptions in rch, rchd, and rch-wkr with owning module references.\n- Mismatch matrix covers at least missing /dp symlink, non-canonical roots, mixed absolute/relative path usage, and stale worker path caches.\n- Each invariant includes a concrete failure example, expected detection signal, and remediation path.\n- Deliverable includes a traceability table mapping each invariant to downstream implementation beads (bd-vvmd.1.2..bd-vvmd.1.6, bd-vvmd.2.1..bd-vvmd.2.4).\n- Review checklist confirms artifact is sufficient to execute implementation without external planning documents.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:01.629129598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:56:50.675108280Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T17:56:50.675085677Z","close_reason":"Completed: path/topology invariant inventory delivered in comment #204; downstream traceability and mismatch matrix included.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","filesystem","foundation","rch-rel-2026q1"],"comments":[{"id":204,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# bd-vvmd.1.1 Deliverable: Path/Topology Invariant Inventory

## 0) Scope and goal
This artifact inventories current path assumptions across `rch`, `rchd`, and `rch-wkr`, defines required filesystem invariants for deterministic remote execution, and records mismatch/failure patterns against the target model:

- canonical project root namespace: `/data/projects/...`
- compatibility alias: `/dp -> /data/projects` symlink on every relevant machine
- remote pre-build verification that required dependency roots/files exist before build execution

This document is intended to be sufficient for downstream implementation without external plan docs.

## 1) Current path-assumption catalog (with ownership)

### A. Hook and CLI assumptions (`rch`)
1. `rch` autostart state dir assumes runtime dir or `/tmp/rch`.
   - Evidence: `rch/src/hook.rs:729`, `rch/src/hook.rs:735`
   - Assumption: `/tmp` is writable/available if `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` is not set.

2. Toolchain detection root is `current_dir()`.
   - Evidence: `rch/src/hook.rs:178`, `rch/src/hook.rs:180`
   - Assumption: invocation CWD is the intended project root.

3. Project identity for daemon selection is derived from CWD basename + hash(CWD string), without canonicalization.
   - Evidence: `rch/src/hook.rs:2088`, `rch/src/hook.rs:2097`, `rch/src/hook.rs:2100`
   - Assumption: string form of CWD is stable and unique for one logical repo.

4. Remote compilation pipeline root is `current_dir()`.
   - Evidence: `rch/src/hook.rs:2283`, `rch/src/hook.rs:2286`, `rch/src/hook.rs:2287`
   - Assumption: transfer/execution root should be exactly invocation CWD.

5. Project config lookup is relative `.rch/config.toml` from CWD.
   - Evidence: `rch/src/config.rs:56`, `rch/src/config.rs:57`
   - Assumption: hook/CLI runs in or below desired project root.

6. State inspection also assumes project config at `cwd/.rch/config.toml`.
   - Evidence: `rch/src/state/mod.rs:370`, `rch/src/state/mod.rs:371`
   - Assumption: relative lookup is sufficient, no canonical/project-root walk needed.

### B. Transfer pipeline assumptions (`rch`)
7. Remote execution path format is `<remote_base>/<project_id>/<project_hash>`.
   - Evidence: `rch/src/transfer.rs:387`, `rch/src/transfer.rs:388`
   - Assumption: this tuple uniquely identifies the logical project state.

8. Remote commands always execute `cd <remote_path> && ...`.
   - Evidence: `rch/src/transfer.rs:423`, `rch/src/transfer.rs:424`
   - Assumption: all needed sources exist under that single remote path.

9. Sync source is only `project_root/` (single-root transfer model).
   - Evidence: `rch/src/transfer.rs:675`, `rch/src/transfer.rs:875`
   - Assumption: required sources are inside one repo root; sibling path deps are not required.

10. `project_hash` includes `project_path.to_string_lossy()` + key-file mtimes.
    - Evidence: `rch/src/transfer.rs:1692`, `rch/src/transfer.rs:1696`, `rch/src/transfer.rs:1717`
    - Assumption: string path identity is stable/canonical across aliases.

11. `project_id` is sanitized basename of path.
    - Evidence: `rch/src/transfer.rs:1788`, `rch/src/transfer.rs:1790`, `rch/src/transfer.rs:1794`
    - Assumption: basename is a safe logical grouping key for remote directories.

### C. Shared config/path primitives (`rch-common`)
12. Default `transfer.remote_base` is `/tmp/rch`.
    - Evidence: `rch-common/src/types.rs:1610`, `rch-common/src/types.rs:1613`, `rch-common/src/types.rs:1931`
    - Assumption: worker scratch/cache should live under `/tmp/rch` unless overridden.

13. `remote_base` validation enforces absolute path, no `..`, not `/`, depth >= 2.
    - Evidence: `rch-common/src/types.rs:1937`, `rch-common/src/types.rs:1942`, `rch-common/src/types.rs:1950`, `rch-common/src/types.rs:1961`, `rch-common/src/types.rs:1969`
    - Assumption: safety of recursive cleanup depends on this contract.

14. Default socket path resolves via runtime/cache/tmp fallbacks.
    - Evidence: `rch-common/src/types.rs:2011`, `rch-common/src/types.rs:2020`, `rch-common/src/types.rs:2027`
    - Assumption: hook and daemon share same effective socket-path resolution.

### D. Daemon assumptions (`rchd`)
15. Daemon cache-cleanup `remote_base` defaults to `/tmp/rch` and is expected to match transfer remote_base.
    - Evidence: `rchd/src/config.rs:135`, `rchd/src/config.rs:160`, `rchd/src/config.rs:306`
    - Assumption: transfer destination and cleanup target are aligned.

16. Cleanup service executes remote `find ... -exec rm -rf` under configured `remote_base`.
    - Evidence: `rchd/src/cache_cleanup.rs:203`, `rchd/src/cache_cleanup.rs:208`
    - Assumption: configured remote_base is precise and safe for recursive deletion.

17. Cache affinity keys are `project_id` strings as provided by hook.
    - Evidence: `rchd/src/selection.rs:113`, `rchd/src/selection.rs:135`, `rchd/src/selection.rs:137`
    - Assumption: project identity is stable across equivalent path aliases.

### E. Worker assumptions (`rch-wkr`)
18. Worker cache base constant is `/tmp/rch`.
    - Evidence: `rch-wkr/src/cache.rs:11`, `rch-wkr/src/cache.rs:165`
    - Assumption: worker cache lifecycle is centered on `/tmp`.

19. Worker executes command in caller-provided `workdir` with no canonical-root guard.
    - Evidence: `rch-wkr/src/main.rs:35`, `rch-wkr/src/main.rs:36`, `rch-wkr/src/executor.rs:20`, `rch-wkr/src/executor.rs:36`
    - Assumption: upstream components only provide valid/safe deterministic paths.

20. Worker disk telemetry probes `/tmp` capacity.
    - Evidence: `rch-wkr/src/main.rs:430`, `rch-wkr/src/main.rs:436`
    - Assumption: `/tmp` pressure is the dominant path for build failures.

### F. Existing symlink primitive available but not wired
21. Idempotent `ensure_symlink(link, target)` already exists.
    - Evidence: `rch/src/state/primitives.rs:305`, `rch/src/state/primitives.rs:321`, `rch/src/state/primitives.rs:343`
    - Assumption: can be reused for `/dp -> /data/projects` enforcement.

## 2) Mismatch matrix vs target `/data/projects` + `/dp` model

| Mismatch | Current behavior | Failure symptom | Detection signal | Repair direction |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Missing `/dp` symlink on host/worker | No global enforcement exists in runtime path bootstrap | Cargo path deps using `/dp/...` fail remotely (`No such file or directory`) | Remote stderr from cargo path-dep resolution; worker preflight absence once added | Implement idempotent topology bootstrap (`/data/projects` + `/dp`) before eligibility (`bd-vvmd.1.3`, `bd-vvmd.1.4`) |
| Non-canonical root usage (`/dp/...` vs `/data/projects/...`) | Hash and project identity are based on raw CWD string | Cache fragmentation, affinity misses, duplicate remote roots for same logical repo | Divergent `project_id`/`project_hash` in logs and duplicate directories under remote_base | Canonical path normalization API and mandatory call sites (`bd-vvmd.1.2`) |
| Mixed absolute/relative root resolution | `.rch/config.toml` and project root derive from runtime CWD | Wrong config loaded or skipped when invoked from subdir/alias path | Missing expected project config in status/doctor outputs; inconsistent behavior by invocation dir | Normalize + explicit project root discovery contract, with diagnostics (`bd-vvmd.1.2`, `bd-vvmd.1.4`) |
| Single-root transfer model for path deps | Rsync syncs only one root (`project_root/`) | Local path deps outside root are absent remotely | Build fails when dependency points to sibling repo path | Add dependency-closure planner + multi-root deterministic manifests/sync (`bd-vvmd.2.1`, `bd-vvmd.2.2`, `bd-vvmd.2.3`) |
| No mandatory pre-build required-file verification | Build starts without validating all dependency roots/files exist remotely | Late failures after transfer/queue time; noisy retries | Build stderr only after command execution; absent early preflight diagnostics | Add remote pre-build verifier before execution (`bd-vvmd.2.4`) |
| Stale worker path caches / cleanup drift risk | Cleanup and transfer depend on matching `remote_base`; cache IDs depend on path strings | Old cache trees linger, inconsistent warm-cache hits, disk growth | Remote disk pressure trends + duplicate project trees across alias hashes | Canonical IDs + remote_base alignment + topology preflight (`bd-vvmd.1.2`, `bd-vvmd.1.4`) |

## 3) Required invariants (failure example, detection, remediation)

### I-1 Canonical workspace identity invariant
- Rule: All logical project roots must canonicalize to one canonical path namespace before identity/hash decisions.
- Prevents: `/dp/foo` and `/data/projects/foo` being treated as different projects.
- Failure example: Same project alternates between two workers with no warm-cache reuse.
- Detection: Compare emitted project identity/hash across equivalent aliases; mismatch is invariant breach.
- Remediation: Central canonicalization utility + mandatory use in hook/transfer/config entry points (`bd-vvmd.1.2`).

### I-2 Topology alias invariant (`/dp -> /data/projects`)
- Rule: Every eligible worker (and host runtime environment) must have `/data/projects` present and `/dp` symlinked to it.
- Prevents: hardcoded `/dp/...` Cargo path dependencies failing remotely.
- Failure example: `/dp/frankentui` dependency missing on worker while `/data/projects/frankentui` exists.
- Detection: worker preflight topology check fails before scheduling.
- Remediation: idempotent bootstrap plus eligibility gate (`bd-vvmd.1.3`, `bd-vvmd.1.4`).

### I-3 Deterministic remote path tuple invariant
- Rule: `{remote_base, project_id, project_hash}` must be deterministic for one logical project state.
- Prevents: duplicate remote trees and cache thrash.
- Failure example: identical commit produces different project hashes because invocation path string differs.
- Detection: identity/hash drift across repeated runs on same commit.
- Remediation: canonicalized root input to `project_id`/`project_hash` computation (`bd-vvmd.1.2`).

### I-4 Remote base alignment invariant
- Rule: `transfer.remote_base`, daemon cleanup remote_base, and worker cache policy must target the same canonical base policy.
- Prevents: cleanup deleting wrong tree or missing actual build/cache directories.
- Failure example: transfer writes under `/var/rch-builds`, cleanup still targets `/tmp/rch`.
- Detection: cleanup stats report success but disk growth continues on actual transfer path.
- Remediation: enforce aligned config + preflight validation + diagnostics (`bd-vvmd.1.4`).

### I-5 Dependency closure invariant for Cargo path deps
- Rule: Full transitive local path-dependency closure must be resolved and represented in deterministic manifest before transfer.
- Prevents: partial sync where only primary repo exists remotely.
- Failure example: workspace compiles locally but remote fails on unresolved sibling path dependency.
- Detection: closure planner reports unresolved/out-of-root dependency; pre-build verifier fails.
- Remediation: resolver + planner + multi-root sync (`bd-vvmd.2.1`, `bd-vvmd.2.2`, `bd-vvmd.2.3`).

### I-6 Pre-build required-files invariant
- Rule: Required dependency roots/files must be verified remotely before executing compilation command.
- Prevents: expensive late build failures after queueing/transfers.
- Failure example: build starts, then fails immediately due to missing `Cargo.toml` in dependent repo root.
- Detection: explicit pre-build verification error code and diagnostics.
- Remediation: remote verifier integrated into selection/launch path (`bd-vvmd.2.4`).

### I-7 Fail-open correctness invariant
- Rule: When closure/verification fails due transient/environmental issues, behavior must remain explicit and safe (fallback semantics preserved).
- Prevents: blocking developer workflow on reliability subsystem failures.
- Failure example: verifier timeout blocks command entirely.
- Detection: command outcome indicates blocked instead of documented fallback.
- Remediation: integrate policy and tests while implementing beads in 2.x chain; ensure diagnostics are explicit.

## 4) Traceability matrix to downstream beads

| Invariant / work item | Primary implementation beads | Test-enforcement beads (minimum) |
|---|---|---|
| I-1 Canonical workspace identity | `bd-vvmd.1.2` | `bd-vvmd.1.6` |
| I-2 `/data/projects` + `/dp` topology alias | `bd-vvmd.1.3`, `bd-vvmd.1.4` | `bd-vvmd.1.6` |
| I-3 Deterministic remote path tuple | `bd-vvmd.1.2`, `bd-vvmd.1.4` | `bd-vvmd.1.6` |
| I-4 Remote base alignment + preflight eligibility | `bd-vvmd.1.4` | `bd-vvmd.1.6` |
| I-5 Cargo path-dependency closure | `bd-vvmd.2.1`, `bd-vvmd.2.2` | `bd-vvmd.2.7`, `bd-vvmd.2.8` |
| I-5 deterministic multi-root transfer realization | `bd-vvmd.2.3` | `bd-vvmd.2.7`, `bd-vvmd.2.8` |
| I-6 pre-build required-files verification | `bd-vvmd.2.4` | `bd-vvmd.2.7`, `bd-vvmd.2.8` |
| I-7 fail-open correctness for unresolved deps | (enforced while implementing `bd-vvmd.2.1..2.4`) | `bd-vvmd.2.9` |

## 5) Suggested execution order (critical path)
1. `bd-vvmd.1.2` canonicalization utility and mandatory call-sites.
2. `bd-vvmd.1.3` bootstrap `/data/projects` + `/dp` idempotently on workers.
3. `bd-vvmd.1.4` gate worker eligibility on topology preflight.
4. `bd-vvmd.2.1` resolver for local path-dependency graph.
5. `bd-vvmd.2.2` closure planner constrained to canonical roots.
6. `bd-vvmd.2.3` deterministic multi-root sync manifests + transfer.
7. `bd-vvmd.2.4` remote required-files verifier before build.
8. `bd-vvmd.1.6` + `bd-vvmd.2.7/2.8/2.9` for exhaustive unit/integration/e2e + logging.

## 6) Review checklist (artifact completeness)
- [x] Enumerates current path assumptions in `rch`, `rchd`, `rch-wkr` with module evidence.
- [x] Includes mismatch matrix with required `/dp` symlink, non-canonical roots, mixed path resolution, stale cache/remote-base drift.
- [x] Defines invariants with failure symptom, detection, and remediation.
- [x] Maps invariants to downstream implementation beads and test-bead enforcement.
- [x] Self-contained for implementation kickoff without external planning doc.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:56:12Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.1.2","title":"Implement canonical path normalization module for local/remote mapping","description":"Implement a shared canonicalization utility that maps equivalent roots (/dp, /data/projects, symlinked variants) to a single canonical identity. Include strict safety checks to prevent escaping canonical root. Acceptance: deterministic canonical path output, rejection of invalid paths, and usage points identified for transfer, dependency closure, and preflight checks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Normalization rules deterministically map equivalent host/worker paths to a single canonical form under `/data/projects`.
- `/dp` symlink aliases are resolved safely and rejected when target invariants are violated.
- Module exposes API used consistently by resolver, planner, transfer, and preflight paths.
- Unit tests cover canonical, alias, invalid, and edge-case path variants with stable expected outputs.
- Structured diagnostics include normalization decision traces for troubleshooting mismatches.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:01.831135217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T18:16:57.491444941Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T18:16:57.491426206Z","close_reason":"Completed canonical path normalization module + integrations; see comment #207 for implementation and validation evidence.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["filesystem","foundation","implementation","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:10.284169626Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":207,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.1.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# bd-vvmd.1.2 completion artifact: canonical path normalization module

## Scope and objective
Implement a shared canonicalization utility that maps equivalent project roots (`/dp`, `/data/projects`, symlinked variants) to a deterministic canonical identity, with strict guardrails and structured diagnostics. Integrate at high-leverage project identity points used for remote transfer and daemon selection paths.

## Implementation summary

### 1) New shared module: `rch-common/src/path_topology.rs`
Added a dedicated topology module with:

- Canonical defaults:
  - `DEFAULT_CANONICAL_PROJECT_ROOT = "/data/projects"`
  - `DEFAULT_ALIAS_PROJECT_ROOT = "/dp"`
- Policy object:
  - `PathTopologyPolicy { canonical_root, alias_root }`
- Normalization APIs:
  - `normalize_project_path(&Path) -> Result<NormalizedProjectPath, PathNormalizationError>`
  - `normalize_project_path_with_policy(&Path, &PathTopologyPolicy) -> Result<...>`
- Deterministic structured decision traces:
  - `NormalizationDecision::{ReceivedInput, VerifiedAbsoluteInput, AliasPrefixDetected, AliasSymlinkVerified, CanonicalRootResolved, CanonicalInputResolved, VerifiedWithinCanonicalRoot}`
- Explicit error model + diagnostics:
  - `PathNormalizationErrorKind::{NotAbsoluteInput, CanonicalRootMissing, CanonicalRootResolveFailed, AliasMissing, AliasNotSymlink, AliasReadLinkFailed, AliasTargetResolveFailed, AliasWrongTarget, InputResolveFailed, OutsideCanonicalRoot}`
  - `PathNormalizationError` with `detail()` and `decision_trace()`

Guardrails enforced by implementation:

- Reject non-absolute input paths.
- Require canonical root to exist and resolve.
- If input starts with alias root, require alias root to exist, be a symlink, and resolve exactly to canonical root.
- Resolve input with `canonicalize` and reject any escape outside canonical root.

### 2) Shared exports from `rch-common`
`rch-common/src/lib.rs` updated to export the topology API and types for reuse by `rch`, `rchd`, `rch-wkr`, and future dependency/planner modules.

### 3) Hook integration (`rch/src/hook.rs`)
Integrated normalization into project identity and transfer startup:

- `extract_project_name()` now:
  - canonicalizes current path with `normalize_project_path`
  - logs full normalization decision trace for observability
  - computes stable `name-short_hash` from canonical path (prevents `/dp/...` vs `/data/projects/...` identity drift)
- `execute_remote_compilation()` now:
  - canonicalizes CWD before deriving `project_id` and `project_hash`
  - passes canonical root into `TransferPipeline::new(...)`
  - emits normalization trace in verbose reporter output

### 4) Diagnose/preflight path alignment (`rch/src/commands/status.rs`)
Aligned simulated worker selection path with hook identity semantics:

- Uses `extract_project_name()` for daemon `project` key so status simulation matches real hook project identity (including canonicalization + hash).
- Keeps normalized canonical root fallback for toolchain detection.

This closes an inconsistency where diagnostic simulations could previously diverge from hook/daemon cache affinity keys.

## Test coverage and verification

### New/updated unit tests
Added exhaustive unit tests in `rch-common/src/path_topology.rs`:

1. `normalize_direct_canonical_path`
2. `normalize_alias_path_to_same_canonical_identity`
3. `reject_relative_path_input`
4. `reject_path_outside_canonical_root`
5. `reject_missing_alias_for_alias_prefixed_input`
6. `reject_alias_pointing_to_wrong_target`
7. `reject_when_canonical_root_missing`

Coverage includes happy-path, alias equivalence, invalid input, invariant violations, and edge conditions with stable expected errors.

### Commands run

- `cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅ (remote attempted first; fail-open local fallback succeeded)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p rch-common path_topology -- --nocapture` ✅ (7/7 tests passed)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p rch test_extract_project_name -- --nocapture` ✅

Known pre-existing workspace lint blocker (not introduced by this bead):

- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ❌
- failure: `clippy::items_after_test_module` at `rch/src/commands/daemon.rs:627`

## Acceptance criteria mapping

- Deterministic canonical mapping under `/data/projects`: ✅
- Safe `/dp` symlink handling with invariant rejection: ✅
- Shared API exposed and used at key transfer + preflight identity paths: ✅
- Unit tests for canonical/alias/invalid/edge cases: ✅
- Structured normalization diagnostics with decision traces: ✅

## Notes for downstream beads

- This bead establishes the canonical path substrate required by:
  - `bd-vvmd.1.3` worker bootstrap enforcement (`/data/projects` + `/dp` invariants)
  - `bd-vvmd.2.1` local path-dependency resolver
  - `bd-vvmd.7.1` deterministic multi-repo fixtures
- Downstream work should reuse `rch_common::normalize_project_path*` directly and avoid bespoke path munging.

-{"id":"bd-vvmd.1.3","title":"Add idempotent worker bootstrap enforcement for /data/projects and /dp symlink","description":"Create bootstrap/enforcement command flow that guarantees /data/projects exists and /dp points to it on each worker. Must be idempotent, safe under repeated runs, and emit clear remediation when enforcement fails. Acceptance: command validates and repairs topology without destructive side effects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bootstrap logic is idempotent and can be rerun safely on already-correct workers.
- Enforcement guarantees `/data/projects` exists and `/dp` points to it without destructive side effects.
- Failure handling distinguishes permission, filesystem, and integrity mismatch causes with remediation hints.
- Worker bootstrap emits structured audit logs for each check/fix decision.
- Integration tests validate repeated runs and partial-failure recovery paths.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:02.028017252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T19:37:53.785369584Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap","foundation","rch-rel-2026q1","workers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:10.489975329Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-vvmd.1.3","title":"Add idempotent worker bootstrap enforcement for /data/projects and /dp symlink","description":"Create bootstrap/enforcement command flow that guarantees /data/projects exists and /dp points to it on each worker. Must be idempotent, safe under repeated runs, and emit clear remediation when enforcement fails. Acceptance: command validates and repairs topology without destructive side effects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bootstrap logic is idempotent and can be rerun safely on already-correct workers.
- Enforcement guarantees `/data/projects` exists and `/dp` points to it without destructive side effects.
- Failure handling distinguishes permission, filesystem, and integrity mismatch causes with remediation hints.
- Worker bootstrap emits structured audit logs for each check/fix decision.
- Integration tests validate repeated runs and partial-failure recovery paths.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:02.028017252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T20:17:24.618224139Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T20:07:35.323329624Z","close_reason":"Implemented remote worker topology bootstrap enforcement (/data/projects + /dp symlink) with structured audit/failure taxonomy plus idempotence/recovery tests and e2e harness coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap","foundation","rch-rel-2026q1","workers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:10.489975329Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":213,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented worker bootstrap topology enforcement in workers setup with per-worker structured audit entries and remediation-classified failure mapping. Added step-0 topology gate before binary/toolchain work, enforcing /data/projects directory and /dp symlink target semantics via remote SSH probes/fixes. Added parse/classification unit coverage plus local idempotence + partial-failure recovery tests (topology_bootstrap_*), and extended tests/e2e/path_dependency_fixtures.sh to execute topology bootstrap suite with explicit logging. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p rch topology_bootstrap_ -- --nocapture; tests/e2e/path_dependency_fixtures.sh; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy -p rch -- -D warnings. Workspace-wide clippy still blocked by pre-existing rch/src/commands/daemon.rs items_after_test_module lint (unchanged).","created_at":"2026-02-16T20:09:18Z"},{"id":214,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Post-implementation hardening pass fixed subtle topology edge cases: (1) remote probes now treat dangling symlinks as existing paths by checking !-e && !-L, avoiding false 'missing' classification and failed create attempts; (2) alias verification now inspects raw readlink target (accepts /data/projects and /data/projects/) instead of readlink -f, preventing canonicalization-induced false mismatches; (3) topology fix audit status now uses explicit enum parameter instead of fragile string matching on action message; (4) failure classifier maps 'file exists'/'not a directory'/'too many levels of symbolic links' to integrity_mismatch for clearer remediation. Revalidated with fmt/check/clippy(targeted) + topology tests + e2e path_dependency_fixtures.","created_at":"2026-02-16T20:17:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.1.4","title":"Gate worker eligibility on filesystem topology preflight","description":"Extend worker capability/preflight checks so workers missing canonical topology are marked ineligible for remote builds until repaired. Must preserve fail-open behavior by allowing local execution fallback. Acceptance: eligibility status reflects real topology state and is visible in diagnostics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Worker preflight gate marks topology-violating workers ineligible before scheduling decisions.
- Eligibility state transitions are deterministic and visible in status/diagnostic surfaces.
- Build execution preserves fail-open local fallback when all workers fail topology checks.
- Repaired workers re-enter service only after explicit preflight revalidation.
- Tests cover healthy, missing-symlink, wrong-target, and flapping-state scenarios.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:02.233915819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:44:23.802675371Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","preflight","rch-rel-2026q1","selection"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:10.691612998Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.1.5","title":"Design migration + rollback plan for existing worker fleets","description":"Produce operator-safe rollout strategy for transitioning existing workers to canonical topology with minimal disruption. Include rollback plan, sequencing, and validation checkpoints. Acceptance: migration plan prevents partial/inconsistent topology states and defines go/no-go criteria.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Migration plan defines phased rollout, rollback triggers, and operator checkpoints per fleet segment.
- Plan includes explicit handling for mixed-state workers during rollout and recovery.
- Validation checklist proves topology invariants before and after each migration phase.
- Rollback procedure restores prior behavior without leaving partial topology state.
- Supporting dry-run procedure and evidence template are included for production readiness reviews.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:02.430988331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:44:24.073559388Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","migration","operations","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:10.894779770Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.1.6","title":"Add unit/integration tests for canonical path and symlink invariants","description":"Create tests covering canonicalization edge cases, symlink correctness, invalid-path rejection, and preflight eligibility gating. Include structured logs for failing cases. Acceptance: deterministic tests across Linux environments and clear diagnostics for invariant violations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover canonicalization edge cases, symlink loops, invalid roots, and permission denials.
- Integration tests validate bootstrap + preflight interactions across realistic worker states.
- Tests assert deterministic rejection/error codes and remediation messages for invariant violations.
- Test logs capture normalized paths, invariant checks, and decision outcomes in machine-readable format.
- CI tier assignment includes fast smoke subset and deeper nightly topology suite.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:02.625248888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:44:24.342945700Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","quality","rch-rel-2026q1","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:11.148800006Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:11.380739100Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","title":"Add E2E scenarios for real cross-repo Cargo path dependencies","description":"Implement end-to-end scenarios for real cross-repo Cargo path dependencies (for example project A depending on project B under `/data/projects`) using the shared reliability harness. Validate successful remote builds after convergence and correct fail-open local fallback when dependencies are unavailable or unverifiable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scenarios cover direct and transitive path dependencies, symlinked `/dp` alias usage, and out-of-root rejection behavior.
- Success-path scenarios verify deterministic remote build success with expected artifact sync.
- Failure-path scenarios verify fallback without local re-execution loops, with explicit reason codes.
- Logs include per-phase closure plan, verification results, worker decision, and fallback rationale.
- Scenarios are integrated into the shared E2E suite and runnable in CI/nightly tiers.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:04.256310490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:18:02.603597667Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","path-deps","rch-rel-2026q1","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:18.602567803Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:17:38.462792646Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:18.806605035Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:13.312412741Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Test-pyramid sequencing tightened: cross-repo path-dependency E2E now depends on edge-case unit coverage (bd-vvmd.2.7) to catch resolver/planner/hash corner cases early and keep E2E diagnostics focused on orchestration-level failures.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:18:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","title":"Add command-classification regression suite to protect non-compilation UX","description":"Add a dedicated regression suite to ensure new reliability logic does not degrade command classification behavior or accidentally intercept commands that must stay local. Focus on preserving user expectations for pass-through behavior while still capturing intended compilation offload cases.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Unit tests cover positive/negative classification for cargo/gcc/clang/make/ninja/bun patterns, including watch/piped/redirected/backgrounded exclusions.\n- Regression tests explicitly assert local pass-through for non-compilation and package-management commands (including bun install/add/remove/link, bun run/build/dev/repl, bunx).\n- Hook-mode integration tests verify fail-open behavior when classifier/preflight timing budgets are exceeded or preconditions are ambiguous.\n- E2E scenarios confirm no unintended remote interception side effects on representative real workflows.\n- Structured logs include classifier rationale, confidence, decision path, and fallback reason codes for every scenario.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:55:04.705571391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:17.143089002Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["classification","e2e","fail-open","rch-rel-2026q1","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:01.129835548Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:01.329762751Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:01.737484699Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:01.533601438Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":174,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Critical user-trust guardrail: this regression suite prevents accidental interception of commands that must stay local. Coverage must include positive compilation detection, explicit non-compilation pass-through, timeout/ambiguity fail-open behavior, and reason-coded decision telemetry so operators can explain every intercept/fallback decision.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3","title":"Workstream: Fleet Repo Convergence via repo_updater Integration","description":"Purpose: integrate `/data/projects/repo_updater` so workers maintain the convex hull of repos required by active/local development graphs. Build a daemon-level convergence service that verifies required repos exist before builds, synchronizes drift safely, and provides operator-visible status/remediation. This reduces missing-repo failures and startup latency for cross-repo local development.

## Success Criteria
- Adapter contract and policy allowlist constrain convergence to trusted roots and deterministic APIs.
- Convergence state machine and selection prechecks prevent missing/stale repo build failures.
- Background drift reconciliation and operator repair flows are available and observable.
- Repo-convergence unit/integration/E2E coverage is complete and deterministic.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:00.627230907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:14.107266701Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["rch","rch-rel-2026q1","repo-sync","repo_updater","workers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:11.793591119Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:21.606944357Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:21.810214112Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:22.098985483Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:36.037914260Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:14.107177544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":132,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Integration principle: use repo_updater as source-of-truth synchronizer for repo presence/freshness, while RCH owns scheduling-time policy decisions. This centralizes sync logic and minimizes custom drift-management code in RCH.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:20:21Z"},{"id":143,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Operational model: workers should hold the convex hull of repos needed by active build demand, not an unbounded mirror of everything. Convergence must be bounded by timeouts/rate limits and policy allowlist, with drift visibility in status surfaces. Pre-build selection checks are required to avoid known-missing-repo failures. If convergence cannot complete safely in budget, fallback locally and surface precise remediation.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:02Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","title":"Define repo_updater adapter contract (commands, JSON schema, errors)","description":"Design stable integration boundary with repo_updater/ru: required commands, expected JSON outputs, failure taxonomy, timeout/retry behavior, and idempotency guarantees. Include version compatibility strategy and fallback behavior if adapter unavailable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Adapter contract specifies command surface, JSON schema, timeout budgets, and retry semantics.
- Contract documents authentication, host validation, and trust boundaries for repo_updater interactions.
- Error mapping normalizes adapter failures into RCH taxonomy with remediation guidance.
- Mockable adapter interface supports deterministic unit/integration tests.
- Contract includes explicit versioning and compatibility expectations for future updates.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:04.457858732Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:06.968196731Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contract","integration","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"]}

Include version compatibility strategy and fallback behavior if adapter unavailable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Adapter contract specifies command surface, JSON schema, timeout budgets, and retry semantics.
- Contract documents authentication, host validation, and trust boundaries for repo_updater interactions.
- Error mapping normalizes adapter failures into RCH taxonomy with remediation guidance.
- Mockable adapter interface supports deterministic unit/integration tests.
- Contract includes explicit versioning and compatibility expectations for future updates.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:04.457858732Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T20:08:08.652638897Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contract","integration","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","title":"Implement daemon RepoConvergence service (convex-hull repo set + drift detection)","description":"Create service that computes required repo hull for active builds, compares worker state, and drives convergence actions through the adapter. Must expose drift state and convergence outcomes for scheduling decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- RepoConvergence service computes and tracks required repo convex-hull state per scheduling context.
- Service maintains explicit drift states with deterministic transitions and hysteresis rules.
- Convergence decisions are bounded by time/attempt budgets to preserve fail-open behavior.
- Service emits structured state-change events for observability and operator tooling.
- Integration tests validate healthy convergence, partial drift, auth failures, and stale-state recovery.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:04.663944951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:07.232540461Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["convergence","daemon","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.599381364Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.397218582Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.484662133Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":149,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RepoConvergence service should expose deterministic states (ready, drifting, converging, failed, stale) with timestamps and reason codes. Keep convergence idempotent and bounded per worker to avoid starvation. Include explicit separation between policy decisions and execution so dry-run/status surfaces can use the same core logic.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","title":"Integrate pre-build repo presence/freshness checks into worker selection flow","description":"Before assigning a worker, ensure required repos exist and meet freshness criteria. If not, trigger bounded sync or select alternate worker; otherwise fallback local per policy. Acceptance: missing-repo failures are prevented proactively.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Worker selection preflight verifies required repos are present and sufficiently fresh before remote build start.
- Preflight consumes convergence state and evidence rather than implicit assumptions.
- Missing/stale repo outcomes trigger explicit local fallback with reason-coded diagnostics.
- Decision logs include repo-level evidence and final selection/fallback rationale.
- Tests validate selection behavior under healthy, drifting, and unknown convergence states.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:04.866291287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:07.507634831Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["preflight","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater","selection"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:26.254572590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.994130539Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.797213178Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:26.455098267Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.4","title":"Add background periodic convergence and drift alerting","description":"Implement background reconcile loop that keeps worker repo hull warm and reports sustained drift. Must avoid interfering with active builds and respect rate limits/time budgets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Background convergence loop runs at controlled cadence with workload-aware throttling.
- Loop avoids interfering with active builds and respects configured safety budgets.
- Sustained drift emits actionable alerts with ranked remediation suggestions.
- Alert noise controls (debounce/hysteresis) prevent flapping false alarms.
- Integration tests cover steady-state, prolonged drift, and recovery-to-healthy transitions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:05.067406758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:07.776122601Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alerts","maintenance","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:15.194944166Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
